intent: Main's README.md is literally one line: "# Resources-" — no stated intent. The real intent lives on unmerged PR #20 branch (claude/high-effort-task-p75njo, pushed today), in link-harvest-design/README.md: "Designunderlag för en återanvändbar process som tar en hög webbläsarlänkar (typiskt ett hundratal åt gången) och omvandlar dem till ett strukturerat kunskapsvalv: en källnot per länk, atomnoter för det mest långlivade innehållet, korslänkar mellan noterna, och tre leverabler per körning" (design basis for a reusable process turning a pile of browser links into a structured knowledge vault). My reading: this IS the intake system Claude proposed — a fully specified, pilot-tested design for a link-triage pipeline, currently a spec-plus-pilot waiting on the owner's four open decisions, with none of its promised code built.

built.schemas: Real and pilot-validated. link-harvest-design/SCHEMAN.md defines exact frontmatter for source notes (tier, tier_reason, value_stage, value_for, half_life, review_by, slop_risk, status, fetch, hash, run_id) and atom notes (concepts/entities, sources provenance, candidate/active status, the half-life split rule). link-harvest-design/RUBRIKER.md defines the S-D tier rubric with hard quotas (max 3 S per 100), half-life-to-review_by mapping, and a slop_risk heuristic. Proven by 15 conforming source notes and 3 atoms under link-harvest-design/pilot-2026-07-21/. No automated validation code exists — a light lint is specified in PIPELINE.md step 9 but unbuilt.
built.ingest: stub: design only, zero code. PIPELINE.md steps 0-3 specify ingest.py (~250 lines), URL normalization, dedup via .state/seen.json, source-type routing, and fetch chains (defuddle -> Skill_Seekers -> WebFetch; arctic-shift for Reddit) — none of these files exist anywhere on any branch (no .py/.sh/.js in the repo at all). The pilot's ingestion was done by hand in a Claude session; the evidence is pilot-2026-07-21/inbox.txt (96 URLs), manifest.json (95 deduped, seed 20260721, 15 sampled), and reddit_fetched.json (40 KB of arctic-shift output).
built.distillation: Executed once manually, not implemented. The pipeline's judgment stages (triage -> S/A calibration -> atom extraction -> distillation, PIPELINE.md steps 4-8) produced real artifacts for the pilot: pilot-2026-07-21/overview.md (tier table for 15 links), tiers.md (verbatim one-line justifications S through D), distillat.md (~2-page synthesis), and 3 extracted atoms (atoms/concepts/EffortCalibration.md, ExternalBrainPattern.md, atoms/entities/MarketingSkillsRepo.md). The promised .claude/skills/link-harvest/ package (SKILL.md, triage-prompt.md, build_overview.py, update_index.py) does not exist.
built.retrieval: none. No vault/index.md, no graph, no search, no seen.json. ROADMAP.md explicitly defers lint/graph to llm-wiki tooling ('drop-in eftersom formaten speglats').
built.ui: none. link-harvest-design/link-harvest-overview.svg (14 KB) is a hand-built system diagram — documentation, not an interface.
built.harness: One real skill on main: .claude/skills/svenska-skrivregler/SKILL.md (178 lines) — complete Swedish typography/anglicism/AI-style rules with a mechanical pre-delivery checklist, not a stub. The link-harvest skill package specified in PIPELINE.md is entirely unbuilt.
built.content: Main: 4 Swedish essays — konsten-att-forhandla.md (negotiation), konsten-att-stalla-fragor.md (art of asking questions), specifikationsproblemet.md (73-line essay on why specification is the bottleneck as AI improves — genuinely good), svenska-skrivregler-for-ai.md (note + paste-ready system-prompt section). Branch: 6 design docs, 15 tier-tagged source notes (mostly r/claudeskills threads + 1 YouTube transcript), 3 atom notes, 4 pilot deliverables, LARDOMAR.md (7 calibration lessons incl. the arctic-shift Reddit fix and the proof that fetch-status and value are separate axes). ~24 knowledge markdown files total across both branches.

unique: Among all the owner's knowledge-system attempts, this is the only one with a value-triage layer that has survived contact with real data: the S-D tier rubric with hard quotas, the half-life split (source note carries the short half-life, extracted atoms carry their own longer one, review_by as a re-tier date not a re-read reminder), and slop_risk as a read-advisory — all exercised on 15 real links with the calibration lessons written down in LARDOMAR.md. llm-wiki defines formats; this repo defines and field-tests judgment. No other attempt should redo the rubric or the pilot.

intent: README, verbatim: "Destillerar Matts Johanssons spridda ChatGPT-trådar om restaurangprojektet i Ljungskile ('Ragu') till strukturerade, spårbara leverabler: konceptbild, affärsplansskelett och budget" — solving, in Matts's own words, "det skapas mycket text i en tråd och det blir svårt att sammanställa detta till ett användbart output." The README's first line also declares its current status: "FRYST BEVISREPO (2026-07-18)... Detta repo är fryst som tidsstämplat bevis på körning två av motorn (Ragu, juni 2026). Ingen vidareutveckling här" — the engine lives on in senseihaiku/loci. My reading: this is not an abandoned attempt but a deliberately frozen evidence snapshot of one complete, successful end-to-end run of a portable "distillation engine" over one project's ChatGPT corpus.

Adapter: /workspace/ljungskile-chatgpt-destillering/scripts/ingest/chatgpt_md.py (87 LOC). Also a separate raw-capture artifact: context/raw/chatgpt-dom-2026-07-22/ — DOM capture of 38 threads (216 messages) as per-thread JSON + assets + manifest.json + checksums.sha256.
built.distillation: The core, fully built and RUN. Pipeline defined in /workspace/ljungskile-chatgpt-destillering/SKILL.md (214 lines): ingest → LLM triage → fan-out extract (one subagent per thread, slices written to disk, only counts returned through context) → LLM reduce (dedup + recency-wins + contradictions preserved) → deterministic assemble (/workspace/ljungskile-chatgpt-destillering/scripts/assemble.py, 159 LOC) → validate → render (/workspace/ljungskile-chatgpt-destillering/scripts/render.py, 536 LOC, schema-driven headings). LLM prompts in /workspace/ljungskile-chatgpt-destillering/reference/prompts.md (275 lines). Zero TODO/FIXME in scripts.

The output is documents, not a queryable base. scripts/validate.py (190 LOC) is a dependency-free JSON-Schema-subset validator + provenance gate (every record needs >=1 anchor with date or quote), which I ran against delivery/result.json today: exit 0, all 1,034 records valid.
built.ui: none as an app. /workspace/ljungskile-chatgpt-destillering/scripts/export_pdf.py (234 LOC, needs Chromium+pypdf) produced two delivered PDFs: delivery/Ljungskile-Ragu-lagesbild.pdf (1.1M) and delivery/Ljungskile-intervju.pdf (96K). reusable: /workspace/ljungskile-chatgpt-destillering/scripts/validate.py — a 190-line dependency-free JSON-Schema-subset validator with provenance enforcement (every record needs an anchor with date or quote) and exit-code contract (0 valid / 1 problems / 2 truncated) used as a gate between every pipeline step; portable to any schema-driven extraction. Caveat: the README claims the engine is being merged into senseihaiku/loci tiro/scripts/ — check there first to avoid harvesting a superseded copy.

The disk-slicing fan-out pattern documented in /workspace/ljungskile-chatgpt-destillering/SKILL.md (Robusthetsregler section) + /workspace/ljungskile-chatgpt-destillering/scripts/assemble.py: extract subagents write slices to disk and return ONLY counts through the context window, then a deterministic stitcher validates-before-write. This is a proven fix for the JSON-truncation failure mode that kills most LLM distillation pipelines, and is directly applicable to the owner's other knowledge-system attempts (llmwiki included).

/workspace/ljungskile-chatgpt-destillering/context/raw/chatgpt-dom-2026-07-22/ capture convention — per-thread JSON + assets/ + manifest.json + manifest.jsonl + checksums.sha256, with honest limitation notes in its README (partial image archive flagged, timestamps derived from conversation IDs). A reusable archival format for ChatGPT DOM exports.
unique: It is the only place holding the complete, checksummed, timestamped record of the Ragu distillation run: raw ChatGPT source threads (29 md + 38 DOM-captured JSON), the validated 1,034-record result.json, the rendered deliverables and the gap interview — plus the steg-2.md reassessment and the 2026-07-22 raw batch that post-date the freeze and exist nowhere else per the repo's own successor note. No other attempt should ever re-run or re-derive the Ljungskile/Ragu extraction; this is that project's evidence base and business memory.

reusable: The height-derivation design in /workspace/destillationssystem/design-spec.md §7.4: salience = 0.6·min(IC,5)/5 + 0.4·min(DD,3)/3 plus the conceptual_reach lift for IC=1 singletons — a worked, empirically motivated fix for 'corroboration-only ranking buries lone high-value insights', with calibration targets (20/17/6 distribution) from reliefkarta.md. Directly portable to llmwiki or any next attempt that needs importance ranking.

The provenance gate recipe in design-spec.md §9.1/§9.1a: GATE-SILVER (quote required, verbatim substring match against read-only source) with an exact copy-pasteable normalization spec (NFC, strip U+FFFC/zero-width, straighten smart quotes, collapse whitespace, ellipsis fragment matching) plus the audit-pass/quarantine pattern §9.1b for substrates without write hooks.

/workspace/destillationssystem/reliefkarta.md wholesale: 121 grains → 44 clusters of real distilled business doctrine (ontologi-först, Spotify-via-mikrofon IP model, stratum-translation, silo-king politics) that exists nowhere else in structured form — it is both preservable content and the calibration dataset for any rebuild.
unique: The two-axis model — tier (refinement: brons/silver/guld) held strictly orthogonal to height (importance DISCOVERED bottom-up via corroboration, never assigned), with a third input (LLM-judged conceptual reach) gated to lone grains — validated against a real 121-grain prospecting run AND adversarially audited against the actual substrate's verified capabilities (honest downgrade of guarantees to "konventionell grind, inte substratspärr"). No other attempt should redo this design thinking; it is finished.

reusable: /workspace/intelligence-layer/specifikation.md — a genuinely tight, domain-neutral 189-line spec for a config-driven distillation engine: contract-as-data (record types, anchored_arrays, labels, options), record+anchor provenance model, script-vs-model division of labor (scripts do bookkeeping, model does judgement), gap-scan → interview → delta-run iteration loop, and resumability invariants (atomic writes, validation gates, checkpoint resume). Directly reusable as the design document for any future knowledge-ingestion attempt.

/workspace/intelligence-layer/org-memory-specifikation.md — cross-project memory spec: shared_arrays subset of anchored records persisted across runs with origin_run provenance, org-scope change resolution, and the Validation Blueprint — a vetting gate that classifies candidate reference material as trusted/excluded/uncertain (with anchored rationale) BEFORE it is used as grounding, routing 'uncertain' to human interview questions.

/workspace/intelligence-layer/context/raw/conversations/README.md + CLAUDE.md session-logging convention — append-only per-session logs named Claude-code_<session-id>.md with timestamped entries; a cheap, working pattern for capturing session output as raw layer.
unique: The Validation Blueprint (org-memory-specifikation.md §7): treating source trustworthiness as a first-class, anchored, persisted record type — every candidate reference document gets a trusted/excluded/uncertain verdict with a traceable rationale before the system is allowed to ground on it, and verdicts are shared across projects so material is vetted once. Every other knowledge-system attempt assumes ingested sources are equally reliable; this is the only repo that specs the "which of these files is actually true" step, and it should not be redesigned from scratch.
